Defendant Lyft, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting The Deposition Of Lyft'S Person Most Qualified, Or In The Alternative Limiting The Scope Of The Topic (Motions)
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALEX D. GUERRERO, Esq. of Mendez & Sanchez, A.P.C. (Tel: 323-838-1444) for plaintiff;
Atty. KELSEY J. MOE, Esq. of Lewisa Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendant Lyft, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting The Deposition Of Lyft's Person Most Qualified, Or In The Alternative Limiting The Scope Of The Topic.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The pro tem judge makes a disclosure to the parties. Neither side objects to the pro tem judge hearing the matter.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion is denied in part and granted in part.

Counsel for parties are instructed to meet and confer on a proposed order which memorializes the Court's rulings at today's hearing, and email an approved proposed order to the pro tem judge by 5pm today at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Arielle B. Farias; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RODNEY PARDILLA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff's "motion for leave to file amended complaint" is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP SEC.428.50(c).) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) No trial date is set. The amended complaint is not "deemed filed."

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
